details.case: {'input': 'What is the average monthly spend by customer segment?', 'actual_output': 'SELECT segment_code, AVG(monthly_spend) as avg_monthly_spend FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 JOIN arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 ON arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288.customer_id = arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336.customer_id GROUP BY segment_code ORDER BY segment_code', 'name': 'Q09', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pa.segment_code, AVG(pm.monthly_spend) AS avg_spend FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa ON pm.customer_id = pa.customer_id GROUP BY pa.segment_code ORDER BY avg_spend DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'join_aggregate'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Both queries use correct tables, correct join logic, and equivalent aggregation. They would return the same data (segment_code and average monthly spend). Minor differences in aliases and ordering don't affect data correctness.

details.case:
{'input': 'Show customers who are in the premium segment and have logged in more than 20 times in the last 30 days.', 'actual_output': "SELECT
  pa.customer_id,
  pa.segment_code,
  ih.total_logins_30d,
  pa.engagement_score,
  pa.lifetime_value_estimate,
  pa.churn_risk_score
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih
  ON pa.customer_id = ih.customer_id
WHERE pa.segment_code = 'premium'
  AND ih.total_logins_30d > 20
ORDER BY ih.total_logins_30d DESC", 'name': 'Q10', 'expected_output': "SELECT pa.customer_id, pa.segment_code, ih.total_logins_30d FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pa.customer_id = ih.customer_id WHERE pa.segment_code = 'premium' AND ih.total_logins_30d > 20 ORDER BY ih.total_logins_30d DESC", 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'two_table_join'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The query uses correct tables, joins, filters, and ordering logic that would return the same core data as expected. However, it includes 3 additional columns (engagement_score, lifetime_value_estimate, churn_risk_score) beyond the 3 required columns, which constitutes significantly more columns than needed.

details.case:
{'input': 'Show retained customers who completed more than 5 lessons and have an NPS score of 8 or higher.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  e.customer_id,
  e.nps_score,
  i.lessons_completed,
  e.retention_flag
FROM arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 e
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 i
  ON e.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE e.retention_flag = 1
  AND i.lessons_completed > 5
  AND e.nps_score >= 8', 'name': 'Q11', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pm.customer_id, ih.lessons_completed, eh.nps_score FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pm.customer_id = ih.customer_id JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pm.customer_id = eh.customer_id WHERE pm.target = 1 AND ih.lessons_completed > 5 AND eh.nps_score >= 8 ORDER BY ih.lessons_completed DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'three_table_join'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The query has correct filters for lessons_completed > 5 and nps_score >= 8, but uses a different method to identify retained customers (retention_flag vs target from performance_metrics table). This could return different results despite having similar logic structure.

details.case:
{'input': 'Calculate the nudge response rate (nudges acted on divided by nudges received) for each customer, sorted highest first.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  customer_id,
  CASE
    WHEN nudges_received = 0 THEN 0.0
    ELSE CAST(nudges_acted_on AS DOUBLE) / CAST(nudges_received AS DOUBLE)
  END AS nudge_response_rate
FROM arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259
ORDER BY nudge_response_rate DESC', 'name': 'Q12', 'expected_output': 'SELECT customer_id, nudges_acted_on, nudges_received, CAST(nudges_acted_on AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(nudges_received, 0) AS response_rate FROM arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ORDER BY response_rate DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'join_calculated'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Both queries use the correct table and calculate nudge response rate correctly. While they handle division by zero differently (CASE vs NULLIF) and have different column selections, they will return equivalent core results sorted correctly. The actual output meets the requirement of calculating nudge response rate for each customer sorted highest first.

details.case:
{'input': 'What is the total monthly spend for customers who have logged in more than 15 times in the last 30 days?', 'actual_output': 'SELECT SUM(pm.monthly_spend) as total_monthly_spend
FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm
INNER JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih
  ON pm.customer_id = ih.customer_id
WHERE ih.total_logins_30d > 15', 'name': 'Q13', 'expected_output': 'SELECT SUM(pm.monthly_spend) AS total_spend, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pm.customer_id = ih.customer_id WHERE ih.total_logins_30d > 15', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'join_sum'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The query uses correct tables, correct join logic, and correct filter (total_logins_30d > 15). It accurately calculates the sum of monthly_spend to answer the question. The expected output includes an extra COUNT column not requested in the original question, making the actual output more precise.

details.case:
{'input': 'Find customers with email open rates above 50% who participated in at least 4 campaigns and have average balances over $5000.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  eh.customer_id,
  eh.email_open_rate,
  eh.campaign_participation_count,
  pm.avg_balance
FROM arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh
JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm
  ON eh.customer_id = pm.customer_id
WHERE eh.email_open_rate > 0.5
  AND eh.campaign_participation_count >= 4
  AND pm.avg_balance > 5000
ORDER BY eh.email_open_rate DESC, pm.avg_balance DESC', 'name': 'Q14', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pm.customer_id, eh.email_open_rate, eh.campaign_participation_count, pm.avg_balance FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pm.customer_id = eh.customer_id WHERE eh.email_open_rate > 0.50 AND eh.campaign_participation_count >= 4 AND pm.avg_balance > 5000 ORDER BY pm.avg_balance DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'multi_table_filter'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The actual SQL uses correct tables, correct filters (email_open_rate > 0.5, campaign_participation_count >= 4, avg_balance > 5000), and equivalent join logic. Both queries would return identical result sets with the same columns. Minor differences in column order and additional ORDER BY criteria don't affect correctness.

details.case: {'input': 'Which customers have a monthly spend higher than the average monthly spend of all customers?', 'actual_output': 'SELECT customer_id, monthly_spend FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 WHERE monthly_spend > (SELECT AVG(monthly_spend) FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288) ORDER BY monthly_spend DESC', 'name': 'Q15', 'expected_output': 'SELECT customer_id, monthly_spend FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 WHERE monthly_spend > (SELECT AVG(monthly_spend) FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288) ORDER BY monthly_spend DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'subquery'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The actual SQL query is identical to the expected SQL query in every aspect - same table, same columns, same filter logic with subquery, and same ordering. Both queries will return exactly the same results.

details.case:
{'input': 'Categorize customers into engagement levels: High (score above 0.7), Medium (0.4 to 0.7), and Low (below 0.4).', 'actual_output': "SELECT
  customer_id,
  engagement_score,
  CASE
    WHEN engagement_score > 0.7 THEN 'High'
    WHEN engagement_score >= 0.4 AND engagement_score <= 0.7 THEN 'Medium'
    WHEN engagement_score < 0.4 THEN 'Low'
  END AS engagement_level
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336
ORDER BY engagement_score DESC", 'name': 'Q16', 'expected_output': "SELECT customer_id, engagement_score, CASE WHEN engagement_score > 0.7 THEN 'High' WHEN engagement_score >= 0.4 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS engagement_level FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 ORDER BY engagement_score DESC", 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'case_statement'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Both queries use the correct table, select the same columns, have identical ordering, and implement logically equivalent CASE logic that will return identical results. The actual output is more explicit in its conditions but functionally equivalent to the expected output.

details.case:
{'input': 'For each customer segment, show the average churn risk score, but only for segments where average monthly spend exceeds $1000.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT 
    pa.segment_code,
    AVG(pa.churn_risk_score) as avg_churn_risk_score,
    AVG(pm.monthly_spend) as avg_monthly_spend
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa
JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm 
    ON pa.customer_id = pm.customer_id
GROUP BY pa.segment_code
HAVING AVG(pm.monthly_spend) > 1000
ORDER BY pa.segment_code', 'name': 'Q17', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pa.segment_code, AVG(pa.churn_risk_score) AS avg_churn_risk, AVG(pm.monthly_spend) AS avg_spend FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm ON pa.customer_id = pm.customer_id GROUP BY pa.segment_code HAVING AVG(pm.monthly_spend) > 1000 ORDER BY avg_churn_risk ASC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'having'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Correct tables, joins, grouping, and filtering logic. Returns equivalent core data (segment and avg churn risk for segments >$1000 spend). Extra avg_monthly_spend column is allowed per rubric. Different ordering doesn't affect data correctness.

details.case:
{'input': 'For each customer, show their churn risk, monthly spend, total logins, and NPS score. Only include customers with churn risk above 0.5.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  p.customer_id,
  p.churn_risk_score,
  pm.monthly_spend,
  ih.total_logins_30d,
  eh.nps_score
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 p
JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm ON p.customer_id = pm.customer_id
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON p.customer_id = ih.customer_id
JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON p.customer_id = eh.customer_id
WHERE p.churn_risk_score > 0.5
ORDER BY p.churn_risk_score DESC', 'name': 'Q18', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pa.customer_id, pa.churn_risk_score, pm.monthly_spend, ih.total_logins_30d, eh.nps_score FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm ON pa.customer_id = pm.customer_id JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pa.customer_id = ih.customer_id JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pa.customer_id = eh.customer_id WHERE pa.churn_risk_score > 0.5 ORDER BY pa.churn_risk_score DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'four_table_join'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Both queries use correct tables, correct filters (churn_risk_score > 0.5), same columns, identical joins, and same ordering. The only difference is table alias naming (p vs pa) which doesn't affect functionality. The queries would return equivalent results.

details.case:
{'input': 'Rank customers by lifetime value within each risk tier.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  risk_tier,
  customer_id,
  lifetime_value_estimate,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY risk_tier ORDER BY lifetime_value_estimate DESC) as rank_within_tier
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336
WHERE risk_tier IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY risk_tier, rank_within_tier', 'name': 'Q19', 'expected_output': 'SELECT customer_id, risk_tier, lifetime_value_estimate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY risk_tier ORDER BY lifetime_value_estimate DESC) AS rank_in_tier FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 ORDER BY risk_tier, rank_in_tier', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'window_function'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The actual SQL uses the correct table, implements the same partitioning and ranking logic, selects the same columns, and would return equivalent results. The additional WHERE clause filtering null risk tiers is a reasonable improvement that doesn't change the core functionality.

details.case:
{'input': 'Identify customers who are churned (target=0) but have above-average engagement scores, suggesting a model discrepancy.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  pm.customer_id,
  pm.target,
  pa.engagement_score,
  pa.churn_risk_score,
  pa.satisfaction_index,
  pm.monthly_spend,
  eh.days_since_last_engagement,
  ih.total_logins_30d
FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm
JOIN arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa ON pm.customer_id = pa.customer_id
JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pm.customer_id = eh.customer_id
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pm.customer_id = ih.customer_id
WHERE pm.target = 0
  AND pa.engagement_score > (SELECT AVG(engagement_score) FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336)
ORDER BY pa.engagement_score DESC', 'name': 'Q20', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pm.customer_id, pm.target, pa.engagement_score, pa.churn_risk_score FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa ON pm.customer_id = pa.customer_id WHERE pm.target = 0 AND pa.engagement_score > (SELECT AVG(engagement_score) FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336) ORDER BY pa.engagement_score DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'complex_business'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: The query has correct core tables, filters, and logic to identify churned customers (target=0) with above-average engagement scores. However, it includes additional unnecessary joins (eh, ih tables) and significantly more columns (8 vs 4) than the expected output, making it more complex than needed.

details.case:
{'input': 'Show customers who are in the premium segment and have logged in more than 20 times in the last 30 days.', 'actual_output': "SELECT
  pa.customer_id,
  pa.segment_code,
  ih.total_logins_30d,
  pa.engagement_score,
  pa.lifetime_value_estimate,
  pa.churn_risk_score
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih
  ON pa.customer_id = ih.customer_id
WHERE pa.segment_code = 'premium'
  AND ih.total_logins_30d > 20
ORDER BY ih.total_logins_30d DESC", 'name': 'Q10', 'expected_output': "SELECT pa.customer_id, pa.segment_code, ih.total_logins_30d FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pa.customer_id = ih.customer_id WHERE pa.segment_code = 'premium' AND ih.total_logins_30d > 20 ORDER BY ih.total_logins_30d DESC", 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'two_table_join'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 3 columns, 3 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'Show retained customers who completed more than 5 lessons and have an NPS score of 8 or higher.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  e.customer_id,
  e.nps_score,
  i.lessons_completed,
  e.retention_flag
FROM arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 e
JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 i
  ON e.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE e.retention_flag = 1
  AND i.lessons_completed > 5
  AND e.nps_score >= 8', 'name': 'Q11', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pm.customer_id, ih.lessons_completed, eh.nps_score FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ih ON pm.customer_id = ih.customer_id JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pm.customer_id = eh.customer_id WHERE pm.target = 1 AND ih.lessons_completed > 5 AND eh.nps_score >= 8 ORDER BY ih.lessons_completed DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'three_table_join'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 3 columns, 5 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'Calculate the nudge response rate (nudges acted on divided by nudges received) for each customer, sorted highest first.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  customer_id,
  CASE
    WHEN nudges_received = 0 THEN 0.0
    ELSE CAST(nudges_acted_on AS DOUBLE) / CAST(nudges_received AS DOUBLE)
  END AS nudge_response_rate
FROM arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259
ORDER BY nudge_response_rate DESC', 'name': 'Q12', 'expected_output': 'SELECT customer_id, nudges_acted_on, nudges_received, CAST(nudges_acted_on AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(nudges_received, 0) AS response_rate FROM arro_poc_interaction_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053259 ORDER BY response_rate DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'join_calculated'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 1 columns, 10 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'What is the total monthly spend for customers who have logged in more than 15 times in the last 30 days?', 'actual_output': 'SELECT SUM(pm.monthly_spend) as total_monthly_spend
FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm

details.case:
{'input': 'Find customers with email open rates above 50% who participated in at least 4 campaigns and have average balances over $5000.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  eh.customer_id,
  eh.email_open_rate,
  eh.campaign_participation_count,
  pm.avg_balance
FROM arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh
JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm
  ON eh.customer_id = pm.customer_id
WHERE eh.email_open_rate > 0.5
  AND eh.campaign_participation_count >= 4
  AND pm.avg_balance > 5000
ORDER BY eh.email_open_rate DESC, pm.avg_balance DESC', 'name': 'Q14', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pm.customer_id, eh.email_open_rate, eh.campaign_participation_count, pm.avg_balance FROM arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm JOIN arro_poc_engagement_history_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053312 eh ON pm.customer_id = eh.customer_id WHERE eh.email_open_rate > 0.50 AND eh.campaign_participation_count >= 4 AND pm.avg_balance > 5000 ORDER BY pm.avg_balance DESC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 3, 'category': 'multi_table_filter'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 4 columns, 4 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'Categorize customers into engagement levels: High (score above 0.7), Medium (0.4 to 0.7), and Low (below 0.4).', 'actual_output': "SELECT
  customer_id,
  engagement_score,
  CASE
    WHEN engagement_score > 0.7 THEN 'High'
    WHEN engagement_score >= 0.4 AND engagement_score <= 0.7 THEN 'Medium'
    WHEN engagement_score < 0.4 THEN 'Low'
  END AS engagement_level
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336
ORDER BY engagement_score DESC", 'name': 'Q16', 'expected_output': "SELECT customer_id, engagement_score, CASE WHEN engagement_score > 0.7 THEN 'High' WHEN engagement_score >= 0.4 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Low' END AS engagement_level FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 ORDER BY engagement_score DESC", 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'case_statement'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 3 columns, 10 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'For each customer segment, show the average churn risk score, but only for segments where average monthly spend exceeds $1000.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT 
    pa.segment_code,
    AVG(pa.churn_risk_score) as avg_churn_risk_score,
    AVG(pm.monthly_spend) as avg_monthly_spend
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa
JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm 
    ON pa.customer_id = pm.customer_id
GROUP BY pa.segment_code
HAVING AVG(pm.monthly_spend) > 1000
ORDER BY pa.segment_code', 'name': 'Q17', 'expected_output': 'SELECT pa.segment_code, AVG(pa.churn_risk_score) AS avg_churn_risk, AVG(pm.monthly_spend) AS avg_spend FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 pa JOIN arro_poc_performance_metrics_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053288 pm ON pa.customer_id = pm.customer_id GROUP BY pa.segment_code HAVING AVG(pm.monthly_spend) > 1000 ORDER BY avg_churn_risk ASC', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'having'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
details.reason: Results match: 1 columns, 2 rows

details.case:
{'input': 'For each customer, show their churn risk, monthly spend, total logins, and NPS score. Only include customers with churn risk above 0.5.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT

details.case:
{'input': 'Rank customers by lifetime value within each risk tier.', 'actual_output': 'SELECT
  risk_tier,
  customer_id,
  lifetime_value_estimate,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY risk_tier ORDER BY lifetime_value_estimate DESC) as rank_within_tier
FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336
WHERE risk_tier IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY risk_tier, rank_within_tier', 'name': 'Q19', 'expected_output': 'SELECT customer_id, risk_tier, lifetime_value_estimate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY risk_tier ORDER BY lifetime_value_estimate DESC) AS rank_in_tier FROM arro_poc_predictive_attrs_iceberg_20260412210737_1776053336 ORDER BY risk_tier, rank_in_tier', 'expected_assertion': None, 'expected_trajectory': None, 'actual_trajectory': None, 'metadata': {'level': 4, 'category': 'window_function'}, 'actual_interactions': None, 'expected_interactions': None, 'actual_environment_state': None, 'expected_environment_state': None}
